description: Fix all test failures across the repo — filesystem, sessions, exec, WASM, network, and Rust crates

userStories.title: Restructure test files to match standardized test layout
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests organized by domain subdirectory so I can find, run, and maintain them easily.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/core/tests/ files are moved into subdirectories: unit/, filesystem/, process/, session/, agents/{pi,claude,opencode,codex}/, wasm/, network/, sidecar/, cron/.
registry/tests/kernel/ is renamed to registry/tests/e2e/ with npm/ and cross-runtime/ subgroups.
crates/execution/tests/javascript.rs is split into javascript/{builtin_interception,module_resolution,env_hardening,sync_rpc}.rs.
Slow sidecar integration tests (crash_isolation, session_isolation, vm_lifecycle, process_isolation) are marked #[ignore].
vitest config globs updated to match new paths.
All tests that passed before the restructure still pass after.
No functional changes — only file moves, renames, and config updates.
userStories.notes: Pure structural refactor. See .agent/specs/test-structure.md for the full plan. Must be done as file moves (git mv) to preserve history. Do not combine with functional changes.

userStories.title: Fix guestFilesystemCall write rejections breaking all sidecar-backed tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want writeFile/mkdir operations through the sidecar VFS RPC bridge to work so that all tests that write files into the VM can run.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/core tests filesystem.test.ts passes (5/5).
packages/core tests network.test.ts passes (1/1).
packages/core tests session-lifecycle.test.ts passes (8/8).
packages/core tests session-comprehensive.test.ts passes (19/19).
packages/core tests acp-protocol.test.ts passes (20/20).
The root cause of 'sidecar rejected request: guestFilesystemCall' on writeFile is identified and fixed.
No test is deleted or disabled to achieve this.
userStories.notes: This is the #1 blocker. ~120 TS tests fail with the same error: NativeSidecarProcessClient.guestFilesystemCall rejects writeFile calls. Tests that only read or test error paths pass. This regression was introduced by the 82 commits from the runtime-isolation-hardening PRD (US-014/015/016 VFS RPC bridge changes). Affects sessions, filesystem, network, process, events — everything that writes to the VM.

userStories.title: Fix 21 Rust JavaScript execution test failures
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all Rust-level JavaScript execution tests to pass so that the JS runtime engine is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo test --package agent-os-execution --test javascript passes with 0 failures (43/43).
Failures span builtin interception, compile cache, process hardening, fs promises routing, TLS/HTTP imports, CJS extensibility, signal handlers, and time freezing.
Do not delete or disable tests.
userStories.notes: 22 pass, 21 fail. Broad failures across many categories — likely multiple root causes from the runtime-isolation-hardening changes.

userStories.title: Fix 11 Rust WASM execution engine test failures
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all Rust-level WASM execution tests to pass so that the WASM runtime engine is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo test --package agent-os-execution --test wasm passes with 0 failures (20/20).
Failures in permission tiers, streaming, warmup/reuse, fuel exhaustion, memory limits, signal state, and symlink target change detection.
Do not delete or disable tests.
userStories.notes: 9 pass, 11 fail. Failures complete in 0.07s suggesting setup/artifact issues for some.

userStories.title: Fix 6 Rust permission_flags test failures
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all permission flag tests to pass so that Node.js permission model enforcement is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo test --package agent-os-execution --test permission_flags passes with 0 failures.
All 6 tests pass: heap limits, WASI permission tiers, runtime memory/fuel limits, worker flags, workspace root exposure, and nested child capabilities.

userStories.title: Fix 3 Rust sidecar test failures
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the sidecar integration test suite to pass fully.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo test --package agent-os-sidecar passes with 0 failures.

Fix: guest_execution_clears_host_env_and_blocks_network_and_escape_paths, vm_resource_limits_cap_active_processes_without_poisoning_followup_execs, execute_scopes_node_permission_flags_to_vm_sandbox_root.

userStories.title: Fix 13 Rust Python execution test failures
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Python/Pyodide execution tests to pass so that the Python runtime is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo test --package agent-os-execution --test python passes with at least 12 of 15 tests passing.
Root causes: heap OOM during Pyodide init, process.binding denial, import-cache race.

userStories.title: Fix remaining TS test failures across process, host-tools, and infra suites
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all remaining TS-level test failures fixed after the VFS write issue (US-300) is resolved.
acceptanceCriteria: spawn-flat-api.test.ts passes (2/2).
shell-flat-api.test.ts passes (1/1).
process-management.test.ts passes (8/8).
all-processes.test.ts passes (3/3).
process-tree.test.ts passes (3/3).
host-tools-prompt.test.ts passes (18/18).
native-sidecar-process.test.ts passes (9/9).
software-projection.test.ts passes (3/3).
agent-os-events.test.ts passes (6/6).
pi-acp-adapter.test.ts passes (2/2).
pi-sdk-adapter.test.ts passes (3/3).
userStories.notes: Many of these may be fixed by US-300. This story covers anything remaining after the VFS write fix.

userStories.title: Fix wasm-commands.test.ts — chmod, permissions, and cross-package pipelines
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all WASM command tests to pass so that shell commands work correctly in the VM.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run tests/wasm-commands.test.ts passes with at least 79/80.
Fix chmod/ls-la permission tests.
Fix cross-package pipeline tests (find|grep|wc, awk+sort, tar+gzip, sed+grep, jq+awk).
userStories.notes: 70/80 passed in a prior run. 9 failures in permissions and multi-binary pipelines. Currently fully skipped (80/80) due to WASM binary detection issue — may need git lfs pull.

userStories.title: Run all tests repo-wide, triage every failure, and iterate until fully green
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every test in the entire repository to pass so the codebase is in a known-good state.
acceptanceCriteria: Run cargo test --workspace. Record every failure.
Run pnpm test (turbo run test across all packages). Record every failure.
For each failure, fix it or document why it is skipped.
Two consecutive full-suite runs produce zero failures.
Final state recorded in progress.txt.
userStories.notes: Capstone story. Cannot pass until all other stories hold under full-suite run.

userStories.title: Set up CI test pipeline, push, and iterate until CI is fully green
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a CI pipeline that runs all tests on every push so that regressions are caught automatically and the main branch stays green.
acceptanceCriteria: A GitHub Actions workflow (e.g., .github/workflows/test.yml) exists that runs on push and PR to the main branch.
CI runs cargo test --workspace for all Rust crates (kernel, execution, sidecar, bridge).
CI runs pnpm test (turbo run test) for all TypeScript packages (packages/core, registry).
CI installs Git LFS and pulls WASM binaries so wasm-commands.test.ts and execute.test.ts are not skipped.
CI builds the Rust sidecar binary (cargo build --package agent-os-sidecar) before running TS tests that depend on it.
CI caches Rust target/, pnpm store, and node_modules for fast reruns.
Push the branch and verify CI runs. If any test fails, fix it and push again. Repeat until CI is fully green.
The final CI run shows 0 failures across all test suites — no test is skipped unless it has a documented, legitimate skip reason (e.g., requires external service like Minio).
CI run time is under 15 minutes.
The workflow is documented in CLAUDE.md under a new 'CI' section.
userStories.notes: This is the final gate. All prior stories fix tests locally; this story ensures they stay fixed by running in CI on every push. The iteration loop is: push -> check CI -> fix failures -> push again until green. CI must test everything: Rust crates, TS packages, WASM commands, registry tests. Git LFS support is required so WASM binaries are available in CI without a native build step.

userStories.title: Add end-to-end adversarial isolation tests for Node.js runtime
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that verify guest Node.js code cannot escape the kernel isolation boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Test that require('fs') returns a kernel-backed polyfill, not real node:fs (verify by checking readFileSync('/') returns kernel VFS root, not host root).
Test that process.env enumeration contains no AGENT_OS_* keys.
Test that require('net'), require('dgram'), require('dns') are either polyfilled or denied.
Test that require('vm'), require('worker_threads'), require('inspector'), require('v8') throw ERR_ACCESS_DENIED.
Test that process.pid returns a kernel PID, not a host PID.
Test that guest stdout cannot inject fake control messages that the sidecar acts on.
All tests run guest JS through the full sidecar pipeline, not just unit-test the loader.
userStories.notes: The most critical gap. .agent/todo/adversarial-isolation-tests.md documents 9 planned stories (US-001–US-009) that are all unimplemented. These tests validate the core security invariants in CLAUDE.md.

userStories.title: Add kernel socket table unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want kernel-level socket table tests so that TCP/UDP/Unix socket lifecycle is verified in isolation from the sidecar.
acceptanceCriteria: New test file crates/kernel/tests/socket_table.rs exists.
Tests cover: socket create, bind, listen, accept, connect (loopback), send/recv, close.
Tests cover: UDP socket create, bind, sendto/recvfrom.
Tests cover: Unix domain socket create, bind, connect.
Tests cover: socket table resource limit enforcement (max sockets).
Tests cover: socket cleanup on process exit.
userStories.notes: Currently zero kernel-level socket tests. Networking is only tested at the sidecar integration level (socket_state_queries.rs).

userStories.title: Add sidecar bridge protocol fuzzing and malformed message tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that send malformed, truncated, and garbage data over the sidecar bridge so that protocol parsing is hardened.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests send malformed JSON over the bridge and verify graceful error response (not crash).
Tests send truncated frames (partial length prefix, partial body) and verify timeout/error.
Tests send binary garbage and verify the bridge recovers or cleanly disconnects.
Tests send valid JSON with unexpected/missing fields and verify rejection.
Tests send oversized payloads exceeding frame limits and verify rejection.
Existing security_hardening.rs oversized-frame test is preserved.
userStories.notes: crates/sidecar/tests/protocol.rs only tests valid codec round-trips. security_hardening.rs has one oversized frame test. No coverage for malformed inputs.

userStories.title: Add dedicated overlay filesystem tests for kernel overlay_fs module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a dedicated test file for overlay_fs.rs so that whiteout, opaque directory, and copy-up logic is verified directly.
acceptanceCriteria: New test file crates/kernel/tests/overlay_fs.rs exists.
Tests cover: whiteout creation on delete, whiteout filtering from readdir results.
Tests cover: opaque directory marking and lower-layer masking.
Tests cover: copy-up on write to lower-layer file.
Tests cover: cross-layer rename atomicity.
Tests cover: read-only lower layer enforcement (writes go to upper only).
Tests cover: metadata root filtering from user-visible results.
userStories.notes: overlay_fs.rs is 1267 lines with complex logic. Currently only tested indirectly through root_fs.rs (9 overlay tests).

userStories.title: Add path traversal and adversarial VFS input tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want kernel VFS tests that attempt path traversal, null byte injection, and other adversarial inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests attempt ../../../etc/passwd traversal through kernel VFS and verify it stays within bounds.
Tests attempt null byte injection in paths (path\x00evil) and verify rejection.
Tests attempt overlong path components (>255 chars) and verify ENAMETOOLONG.
Tests attempt symlink chains exceeding ELOOP limit and verify ELOOP error.
Tests attempt TOCTOU: create symlink, check permission, replace symlink target, verify operation uses resolved path.
All tests run at the kernel crate level (crates/kernel/tests/).
userStories.notes: TS-side host-dir-backend.test.ts has one ../../etc/passwd test. Kernel VFS has zero adversarial path tests.

userStories.title: Add resource exhaustion stress tests under adversarial load
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that simulate adversarial resource exhaustion (fork bombs, FD floods, inode flooding) to verify limits hold under pressure.
acceptanceCriteria: Test spawns processes in a tight loop up to max_processes limit, verifies the limit holds and no OOM/panic occurs.
Test opens FDs in a loop up to max_fds limit, verifies EMFILE after limit.
Test creates files in a loop up to max_inodes limit, verifies ENOSPC after limit.
Test writes data up to max_filesystem_bytes limit, verifies ENOSPC after limit.
Tests run concurrently (multiple goroutines/tasks) to test race conditions in resource accounting.
Existing resource_accounting.rs tests (small thresholds) are preserved.
userStories.notes: resource_accounting.rs tests limits with small thresholds (1 process, 6 FDs) but never tests concurrent adversarial pressure.
